narrative: Dear [Student's Name], as a Black woman applying to law school, you've shown determination in pursuing your dream despite the challenges many face in this field, and we're here to help you understand the admission decision clearly and empathetically. Your profile includes a Year 1 school decile of 5, a Year 3 decile of 4, an undergraduate GPA of 3.0, an LSAT score of 35, upper-middle family income, and plans to attend full-time—solid foundations, but with some areas below typical averages like the dataset's Year 1 decile of 5.71, Year 3 of 5.53, GPA of 3.21, and LSAT of 36.7. The admissions model, trained on past students' data to predict first-time bar exam success, estimated a 72.8% chance you'll face failure on that exam, which directly led to your application's rejection to protect the school's outcomes. The biggest factor pushing toward this prediction was your Year 3 school decile, as being in the 4th decile—lower than most—strongly signaled higher risk of bar struggles compared to typical students. Next, your undergraduate GPA of 3.0 had a substantial negative pull, falling short of the average and reinforcing concerns about sustaining the rigor ahead. Your Year 1 decile of 5 also contributed unfavorably, though less intensely, as it too sat below the norm. On a brighter note, your LSAT score nudged things slightly toward success despite being under average, and your full-time status helped a bit since most successful students share that path. Family income played a minor role in the risk direction. Compared to typical admitted students who pass the bar, your grades across the board were the key gaps, outweighing other strengths. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT or bolstering your GPA through post-bac courses, while targeting schools with strong support for Black women in law—many thrive with targeted prep, and your upper-middle background gives you resources to build on. You've got resilience; use this feedback to strengthen those core academic markers and reapply stronger. We're rooting for your success.
